record_office_terminology.marriage_licenses: County Clerk issues marriage licenses. Nevada is known for easy marriage — no waiting period, no blood test.
record_office_terminology.vital_records_state_start: 1911 (state registration). County clerks recorded marriages and some vital events before 1911. Birth and death records are spotty before state registration.

burned_county_authority: FamilySearch Research Wiki — Nevada Record Losses; Nevada State Archives guides. Nevada has no burned counties, but territorial-era records are sparse in remote mining counties.

burned_counties_summary.notes: Nevada has no burned counties. Record survival is generally good for county courthouse records. However, remote mining camps, ghost towns, and boom-bust population cycles mean records outside county seats are often incomplete or scattered.

administrative_notes: Nevada Territory was organized in 1861 from western Utah Territory and became the 36th state on October 31, 1864 — 'Battle Born' during the Civil War. The Comstock Lode silver discovery (1859) drove rapid settlement and statehood. Originally 9 counties were created in 1861; the current 16 counties plus Carson City (independent city since 1969) form 17 total jurisdictions. Ormsby County was abolished in 1969 when it merged with Carson City. Nevada's population has been highly transient due to mining booms, railroad construction, military bases, and the gaming/entertainment industry. The Nevada State Library, Archives and Public Records in Carson City is the central state repository. County recorders handle land records; county clerks handle marriage licenses; the state registrar (from 1911) handles vital records.

core_repositories.source_name: Nevada State Library, Archives and Public Records
core_repositories.description: Central state repository holding territorial and state government records from 1861, governor's papers, legislative records, state agency files, and transferred county records. Houses the Nevada State Archives which preserves pre-statehood territorial records, early county records, and state vital records.

Nevada's oldest cultural institution (founded 1904). Holds manuscript collections, photograph archives, maps, mining company records, and personal papers covering all of Nevada's history. Research library has extensive genealogical materials including family files and Nevada newspaper indexes.

core_repositories.source_name: University of Nevada, Reno — Special Collections and University Archives
core_repositories.description: Major research collection for Nevada history including mining company records, Basque studies materials, oral histories, political papers, and manuscript groups. The Center for Basque Studies is housed here.
Strong collection for southern Nevada and Las Vegas history including gaming industry records, hotel/entertainment records, Henderson industrial history, and Clark County development materials.
Federal records for Nevada including BLM land patent records, Chinese Exclusion Act case files, BIA records for Nevada tribal communities, federal court records, and military installation records.

familysearch_collections.description: Microfilmed and digitized Nevada county records including marriage records, vital records, probate files, and land records from territorial era through mid-20th century. Coverage varies by county but many counties have marriage records from the 1860s.
Indexed death records from state registration. Includes name, date, location, and other details.

newspapers_and_periodicals.description: Digitized historic Nevada newspapers including the Territorial Enterprise (Virginia City), Reese River Reveille (Austin), Goldfield News, Tonopah Bonanza, Reno Gazette-Journal, and other territorial and statehood-era papers.

newspapers_and_periodicals.source_name: Nevada Newspaper Project — Nevada State Library
newspapers_and_periodicals.description: Microfilm collection of Nevada newspapers not yet digitized. Covers smaller mining camp newspapers, rural weeklies, and papers from ghost towns that are not available online.

military_records.description: State militia records, Civil War volunteer enlistments (Nevada contributed troops despite being a territory/new state), National Guard records, and WWII service records. Nevada had relatively small military contingents but contributed significantly during the Civil War and both World Wars.

military_records.source_name: Fort Churchill, Nellis AFB, and Nevada Military Installation Records
military_records.description: Federal military records from Nevada installations including Fort Churchill (1860–1869), Nellis AFB (Las Vegas), Fallon NAS, Hawthorne Army Depot, and Tonopah Test Range. Personnel records, post returns, and civilian employee files.

land_and_property.source_name: BLM General Land Office Records — Nevada
land_and_property.repository: Bureau of Land Management / GLO Records
land_and_property.description: Federal land patents for Nevada including homestead entries, mineral entries, and mining claims on federal land. Nevada is predominantly federal land (over 80%), making BLM records essential. Searchable online database.

Each of Nevada's 16 county recorders and Carson City Recorder maintains deed records, mining claims, mortgages, and related land instruments. Online access varies widely — Clark and Washoe counties have good online systems; rural counties generally require in-person visits.

vital_records.source_name: Nevada Division of Public and Behavioral Health — Vital Records
vital_records.repository: Office of Vital Records, Carson City
vital_records.description: State vital records from 1911 (state registration). Birth and death certificates available for qualifying applicants. Pre-1911 records are scattered among county clerks and are incomplete.

County clerks have issued marriage licenses since territorial era. Nevada's liberal marriage laws made it a destination for weddings (Clark County) and remarriage (Washoe County after divorce). Some counties have records from the 1860s.

church_and_cemetery.description: Cemetery records and burial transcriptions for Nevada cemeteries including ghost town cemeteries, mining camp burial grounds, and municipal cemeteries. Coverage varies but growing — important for remote areas where other records are sparse.

church_and_cemetery.source_name: LDS Church Records — Southern Nevada Wards
church_and_cemetery.repository: Church History Library, Salt Lake City / FamilySearch
church_and_cemetery.description: Ward records for LDS communities in Lincoln, Clark, Nye, and White Pine counties. Panaca Ward (1864) is among the earliest organized congregations in Nevada. Membership, baptisms, marriages, blessings.

research_guides_and_societies.description: Statewide genealogical society with publications, workshops, and research assistance. Publishes 'The Sagebrush Searcher' newsletter with Nevada-specific research articles.
Comprehensive research guide for Nevada genealogy including county-by-county record availability, record loss information, historical background, and links to online resources.
Southern Nevada genealogical society with research assistance, published indexes, and meetings. Focuses on Clark County records and Las Vegas-area research.

historical_counties_extinct.notes: Ormsby County was one of Nevada's original nine counties, created in 1861 and named for Maj. William Ormsby, killed in the Pyramid Lake War. It was dissolved in 1969 when it merged with Carson City to form a consolidated municipality — the only independent city in Nevada. All Ormsby County records were transferred to Carson City. Researchers must search under 'Ormsby County' for pre-1969 records.
historical_counties_extinct.records_location: Carson City Recorder and Clerk offices hold former Ormsby County records. Nevada State Archives also has territorial and early Ormsby County records.

historical_counties_extinct.notes: Bullfrog County was briefly created from Nye County in 1987 in an attempt to manage the proposed Yucca Mountain nuclear waste repository, but was declared unconstitutional and dissolved in 1989. It never had any independent record-keeping. All records remain in Nye County.
St. Mary's was one of the original nine counties created in 1861 but was renamed Lander County in 1862. No separate records exist under the St. Mary's name.

historical_counties_extinct.successor: Roop County (1862), then to Washoe County (1883)
historical_counties_extinct.notes: Lake County was one of the original nine counties, renamed Roop County in 1862. Roop County was absorbed by Washoe County in 1883. The Honey Lake area straddled the California-Nevada border, and jurisdiction was disputed until the boundary was finalized.
historical_counties_extinct.records_location: Washoe County, Reno. Some records may also be in Lassen County, California.

native_american_and_removal_records.source_name: Bureau of Indian Affairs — Nevada Agency Records
native_american_and_removal_records.description: BIA records for Nevada's tribal communities including the Paiute (Northern and Southern), Western Shoshone, and Washoe peoples. Includes agency correspondence, census rolls, allotment records, and school records. Key agencies include the Western Shoshone Agency, Nevada Agency (Carson City), and Walker River Agency.

native_american_and_removal_records.repository: NARA San Bruno / Nevada Indian Commission
native_american_and_removal_records.description: Student enrollment records, academic files, and photographs from the Stewart Indian School in Carson City, which served Native American students from Nevada and neighboring states for 90 years. Paiute, Shoshone, and Washoe students are documented.

native_american_and_removal_records.repository: UNR Special Collections / Nevada State Library
native_american_and_removal_records.description: Records from the Inter-Tribal Council of Nevada documenting tribal governance, land claims (Western Shoshone claims are particularly significant), and community programs across multiple Nevada tribal nations.

immigration_and_naturalization.source_name: Chinese Exclusion Act Case Files — Nevada
immigration_and_naturalization.description: Case files from enforcement of the Chinese Exclusion Act in Nevada (1882–1943). Documents Chinese miners, railroad workers, merchants, and laborers in Virginia City, Elko, Winnemucca, and other communities. Includes photographs, affidavits, and testimony.

immigration_and_naturalization.source_name: Center for Basque Studies — Basque Immigration Records
immigration_and_naturalization.description: Research center documenting Basque immigration to the American West. Holds oral histories, boarding house records, family histories, and community organization files. Basque sheepherders and ranchers settled across northern Nevada from the 1880s, concentrated in Elko, Humboldt, and Washoe counties.

immigration_and_naturalization.repository: Nevada State Archives / Individual county courts
immigration_and_naturalization.description: Naturalization petitions, declarations of intention, and certificates filed in Nevada county courts. Documents Cornish, Irish, Italian, Chinese, Basque, Greek, Serbian, and other immigrant communities. Older records transferred to state archives.
